If this field is empty, traffic will be: If this field is empty, traffic will be allowed from all external sources.

Sources added to this rule will allow tr: Sources added to this rule will allow traffic to the pods defined above. Sources in this list are combined using a logical OR operation.
Destinations added to this rule will all: Destinations added to this rule will allow traffic from the pods defined above. Destinations in this list are combined using a logical OR operation.
This action cannot be undone. Deleting a: This action cannot be undone. Deleting a node will instruct Kubernetes that the node is down or unrecoverable and delete all pods scheduled to that node. If the node is still running but unresponsive and the node is deleted, stateful workloads and persistent volumes may suffer corruption or data loss. Only delete a node that you have confirmed is completely stopped and cannot be restored.
Unschedulable nodes won't accept new pod: Unschedulable nodes won't accept new pods. This is useful for scheduling maintenance or preparing to decommission a node.
{{ machineHealthCheckLabelPlural }} auto: {{ machineHealthCheckLabelPlural }} automatically remediate node health issues.
Only one {{ machineHealthCheckLabel }} r: Only one {{ machineHealthCheckLabel }} resource should match this node.
This node's {{conditionDescription}}. Pe: This node's {{conditionDescription}}. Performance may be degraded.
This node has requested to join the clus: This node has requested to join the cluster. After approving its certificate signing request the node will begin running workloads.
This node has a pending server certifica: This node has a pending server certificate signing request. Approve the request to enable all networking functionality on this node.
An error occurred. Please try again: An error occurred. Please try again
No new Pods or workloads will be placed: No new Pods or workloads will be placed on this Node until it's marked as schedulable.
Identity providers determine how users l: Identity providers determine how users log into the cluster.
Authentication is being reconfigured. Th: Authentication is being reconfigured. The new identity provider will be available once reconfiguration is complete.
View authentication conditions for recon: View authentication conditions for reconfiguration status.
An eviction is allowed if at most \"maxU: An eviction is allowed if at most "maxUnavailable" pods selected by "selector" are unavailable after the eviction, i.e. even in absence of the evicted pod. For example, one can prevent all voluntary evictions by specifying 0. This is a mutually exclusive setting with "minAvailable".
An eviction is allowed if at least \"min: An eviction is allowed if at least "minAvailable" pods selected by "selector" will still be available after the eviction, i.e. even in the absence of the evicted pod. So for example you can prevent all voluntary evictions by specifying "100%".
Are you sure you want to remove the PodD: Are you sure you want to remove the PodDisruptionBudget <1>{{pdbName}}</1> from <4>{{workloadName}}</4>?
Label query over pods whose evictions ar: Label query over pods whose evictions are managed by the disruption budget. Anull selector will match no pods, while an empty ({}) selector will select all pods within the namespace.
Resource is already covered by another P: Resource is already covered by another PodDisruptionBudget
A maxUnavailable of 0% or 0 or a minAvai: A maxUnavailable of 0% or 0 or a minAvailable of 100% or greater than or equal to the number of replicas is permitted but can block nodes from being drained.
